goal: Synthetic 200+ node board for viewer v2 performance baseline smoke tests

tasks.title: Seed viewer v2 performance baseline plan
tasks.acceptance: Large-board performance fixture scope is accepted

tasks.title: Awaiting user decision 1 for large-board routing
tasks.acceptance: User answers synthetic decision gate 1

decision_package.context_md: The synthetic viewer-performance board has reached epic 6. This gate models a user-owned sequencing decision that keeps large-board awaiting-user rendering realistic.
decision_package.question: For D01, should the next epic prioritize breadth-first parallel cleanup or the critical-path integration slice?
decision_package.what_i_need: Choose one option so downstream blocked nodes can be re-sequenced.
decision_package.why_it_matters: The choice changes which ready work becomes dispatchable first and gives the viewer several realistic user-gated nodes to render.

options.label: Critical path first
options.rationale: Shortens the longest dependency chain before expanding parallel cleanup.
options.tradeoffs: Improves makespan; leaves some fill work for later.

options.label: Breadth first
options.rationale: Keeps more agents busy across independent branches.
options.tradeoffs: Improves utilization; may not shorten the critical path immediately.

tasks.acceptance: All viewer v2 baseline paths remain responsive on the 200+ node board

judgment_calls.summary: Use a synthetic fixture instead of copying a real dogfood board
judgment_calls.decision: Generate a deterministic public-safe board with realistic graph pressure.
judgment_calls.rationale: The fixture needs scale and awaiting-user coverage without leaking project-specific data.
judgment_calls.impact: Viewer v2 can use a stable baseline while avoiding provenance risk.

log.summary: Synthetic completed epics model historical throughput.
Six awaiting-user decision gates added for viewer rendering coverage.
